content:
NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "And in the moment when the sound ‘Om’ touched his ear, Siddhartha’s dormant spirit suddenly awoke and he recognized the folly of his action."
  • Reflection: What sound, sight, or feeling brought me back to my center today?
Day 18
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "He slept a sleep of the most profound kind, a sleep without dreams... When he awoke, he felt as if ten years had passed."
  • Today's Intention: I will try to see someone I know well with fresh eyes today, as if for the first time.
NIGHT
  • Night Reading: "Govinda looked at the sleeper with concern, and yet with a feeling of love and attraction. He did not know this man, and yet he seemed familiar to him; his heart was drawn to him."
  • Reflection: How have I changed? If a friend from 10 years ago saw me today, what would they recognize, and what would seem new?
Day 19
MORNING
  • Morning Reading: "‘It is a beautiful river,’ he said to his companion. ‘Yes,’ said the ferryman, ‘a very beautiful river. I love it more than anything. I have often listened to it, I have often looked into its eyes, and I have always learned something from it.’"
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 20 (chunk 20/29)
